text.title: FinOps — Reserved Instances / Savings Plans Commitment Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the fco-commitment-pricing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Commitment recommendation MUST be backed by at least 3 months of utilization baseline; commitments made on < 3 months data are forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS FinOps best practices, FinOps Foundation framework

rule.rationale: Insufficient baseline produces over-commitment that locks in waste for 1-3 years; the 3-month floor is the empirical minimum.

rule.statement: When workload mix may shift (instance family change, region change) within the commitment term, plan MUST select Convertible RIs / Compute Savings Plan rather than Standard RIs.
rule.rationale: Standard RIs are non-modifiable; convertibles exchange flexibility for ~3pp lower discount.

rule.statement: Commitment MUST cover the BASE load (e.g. p25 hourly utilization), NOT peak; over-commitment of peak yields unused-commitment waste.
rule.rationale: Commitments are paid whether utilized or not; covering peak guarantees waste during the long tail when usage is below peak.

rule.statement: Plan MUST emit a metric `commitment_utilization_pct` and alert when it drops below 80% for 7 consecutive days.

rationale.source: AWS Cost Explorer docs, FinOps Foundation

rule.rationale: Unused commitment is real cash burn; the alert is the only mechanism to detect drift before the term ends.

rule.statement: Commitment terms MUST be staggered (not all expiring same month); a single-renewal-cliff exposes the team to pricing risk.
rule.rationale: Coincident expirations leave the team renegotiating at provider-favorable timing; staggered terms smooth the negotiation.

rule.statement: If the workload is bursty or pre-product, this methodology MUST be skipped — apply `fco-spot-instances` or wait for stability.
rule.rationale: Commitments require predictability; without it, the discount is overwhelmed by unused-commitment waste.
